elements.short: Describes validation requirements, source(s), status and dates for one or more elements
elements.definition: Describes validation requirements, source(s), status and dates for one or more elements.

elements.short: Logical id of this artifact
elements.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

elements.short: Metadata about the resource
elements.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

elements.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
elements.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

elements.short: Language of the resource content
elements.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.

elements.short: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation
elements.definition: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

elements.short: Contained, inline Resources
elements.definition: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: A resource that was validated
elements.definition: A resource that was validated.

elements.short: The fhirpath location(s) within the resource that was validated
elements.definition: The fhirpath location(s) within the resource that was validated.

elements.short: none | initial | periodic
elements.definition: The frequency with which the target must be validated (none; initial; periodic).

elements.short: attested | validated | in-process | req-revalid | val-fail | reval-fail
elements.definition: The validation status of the target (attested; validated; in process; requires revalidation; validation failed; revalidation failed).

elements.short: When the validation status was updated
elements.definition: When the validation status was updated.

elements.short: nothing | primary | multiple
elements.definition: What the target is validated against (nothing; primary source; multiple sources).

elements.short: The primary process by which the target is validated (edit check; value set; primary source; multiple sources; standalone; in context)
elements.definition: The primary process by which the target is validated (edit check; value set; primary source; multiple sources; standalone; in context).

elements.short: Frequency of revalidation
elements.definition: Frequency of revalidation.

elements.short: The date/time validation was last completed (including failed validations)
elements.definition: The date/time validation was last completed (including failed validations).

elements.short: The date when target is next validated, if appropriate
elements.definition: The date when target is next validated, if appropriate.

elements.short: fatal | warn | rec-only | none
elements.definition: The result if validation fails (fatal; warning; record only; none).

elements.short: Information about the primary source(s) involved in validation
elements.definition: Information about the primary source(s) involved in validation.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Reference to the primary source
elements.definition: Reference to the primary source.

elements.short: Type of primary source (License Board; Primary Education; Continuing Education; Postal Service; Relationship owner; Registration Authority; legal source; issuing source; authoritative source)
elements.definition: Type of primary source (License Board; Primary Education; Continuing Education; Postal Service; Relationship owner; Registration Authority; legal source; issuing source; authoritative source).

elements.short: Method for exchanging information with the primary source
elements.definition: Method for communicating with the primary source (manual; API; Push).

elements.short: successful | failed | unknown
elements.definition: Status of the validation of the target against the primary source (successful; failed; unknown).

elements.short: When the target was validated against the primary source
elements.definition: When the target was validated against the primary source.

elements.short: yes | no | undetermined
elements.definition: Ability of the primary source to push updates/alerts (yes; no; undetermined).

elements.short: specific | any | source
elements.definition: Type of alerts/updates the primary source can send (specific requested changes; any changes; as defined by source).

elements.short: Information about the entity attesting to information
elements.definition: Information about the entity attesting to information.

elements.short: The individual or organization attesting to information
elements.definition: The individual or organization attesting to information.

elements.short: When the who is asserting on behalf of another (organization or individual)
elements.definition: When the who is asserting on behalf of another (organization or individual).

elements.short: The method by which attested information was submitted/retrieved
elements.definition: The method by which attested information was submitted/retrieved (manual; API; Push).

elements.short: The date the information was attested to
elements.definition: The date the information was attested to.

elements.short: A digital identity certificate associated with the attestation source
elements.definition: A digital identity certificate associated with the attestation source.

elements.short: A digital identity certificate associated with the proxy entity submitting attested information on behalf of the attestation source
elements.definition: A digital identity certificate associated with the proxy entity submitting attested information on behalf of the attestation source.

elements.short: Proxy signature
elements.definition: Signed assertion by the proxy entity indicating that they have the right to submit attested information on behalf of the attestation source.

elements.short: Attester signature
elements.definition: Signed assertion by the attestation source that they have attested to the information.
